title: Autonomous Software Development with MetaGPT
modules: Multi-Agent Software Engineering: What MetaGPT Does and Where It Fits
Role Architecture: Product Manager, Architect, Engineer, and QA Agents
Translating Requirements into Agent Workflows: From Brief to Code
Reviewing, Correcting, and Auditing AI-Generated Codebases
Integrating MetaGPT into Existing Development Pipelines and CI/CD
Failure Modes, Hallucinated APIs, and When to Override the Agents
Governance and Accountability in Autonomous Code Generation
Running a Real Project: End-to-End with MetaGPT in Your Work Context
synopsis: MetaGPT operationalizes a full software development team as a coordinated multi-agent system — assigning roles, producing structured artifacts, and generating working code from natural language requirements. This course is designed for senior developers, engineering managers, and technical leads who want to understand what autonomous software development actually delivers today, how to integrate it responsibly, and where human oversight remains non-negotiable.
rationale: Multi-agent code generation tools are moving from research demonstrations to real development pipelines, and technical leaders who cannot critically evaluate their outputs and failure modes risk shipping AI-generated code without adequate oversight.
learning_outcome: Learners will be able to configure and run a MetaGPT-assisted development workflow, critically audit its outputs for correctness and security, and define the governance boundaries required for responsible use in a production engineering environment.
source_signals: OpenCode – Open source AI coding agent (score: 3131)
Open source AI is the path forward (score: 5021)
